name: Jimmy Jones
abstract: James Henry Jones (December 30, 1918  – April 29, 1982) was an American jazz pianist and arranger.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert with Sarah Vaughan, Erroll Garner, Lester Young and Others

quotes.text: I had one thing and I lost it in a fire and I'm so sorry, but that's one of the reasons I -- I had the original "Black, Brown, and Beige" off the Carnegie Hall stage.
quotes.caption: Mentions losing original 'Black, Brown, and Beige' recording

about_quotes.speaker: John Malachi
about_quotes.evidence: Well, Jimmy Jones was a friend of mine, and had been a friend of mine since back during the Chicago days... we were good friends, and we had just a real piano comrade thing going.

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: I brought in Jimmy Jones to take his place, because I had worked with Jimmy Jones and knew him well. I’d worked with him with Tony Fambrough’s orchestra. We’d done dates together.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: Then I did some things with Trummy Young which John Levy did before he became George Shearing's manager and Jimmy Jones and Ray Nance... Jimmy Jones made the arrangements.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: she came in with Jimmy Jones, and so they used our rhythm section... I did a concert this past Sunday and in my group I had Jimmy Jones, piano

about_quotes.speaker: Nancy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: But when you have the arrangers that I had, who wrote such beautiful charts – Jimmy Jones – it was just easy, a piece of cake.
about_quotes.doc_title: Nancy Wilson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Levy
about_quotes.evidence: And then Jimmy Jones and I had been friends, I knew him as a player and I recommended that he come on with us.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Levy interviewed by Monk Rowe, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995
